Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse 523 upvotes, 72 comments
  2. Optimizing Top K in Postgres 100 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. Show HN: Remotely use my guitar tuner 218 upvotes, 48 comments
  4. I put my whole life into a single database 98 upvotes, 21 comments
  5. An opinionated take on how to do important research that matters 137 upvotes, 35 comments
  6. JSLinux Now Supports x86_64 340 upvotes, 105 comments
  7. Darkrealms BBS 112 upvotes, 29 comments
  8. Graphing how the 10k* most common English words define each other 83 upvotes, 20 comments
  9. Practical Guide to Bare Metal C++ 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" 82 upvotes, 20 comments
  11. Two Years of Emacs Solo 280 upvotes, 88 comments
  12. LoGeR – 3D reconstruction from extremely long videos (DeepMind, UC Berkeley) 75 upvotes, 19 comments
  13. Lotus 1-2-3 on the PC with DOS 120 upvotes, 39 comments
  14. TCXO Failure Analysis 35 upvotes, 8 comments
  15. Getting Started in Common Lisp 57 upvotes, 16 comments
  16. Ask HN: Remember Fidonet? 26 upvotes, 7 comments
  17. FreeBSD 14.4-Release Announcement 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  18. EVi, a Hard-Fork of Vim 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  19. The hidden compile-time cost of C++26 reflection 39 upvotes, 15 comments
  20. The “JVG algorithm” only wins on tiny numbers 72 upvotes, 39 comments
  21. No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user 272 upvotes, 198 comments
  22. Launch HN: Terminal Use (YC W26) – Vercel for filesystem-based agents 107 upvotes, 74 comments
  23. macOS Tahoe windows have different corner radiuses 197 upvotes, 150 comments
  24. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 198 upvotes, 163 comments
  25. Learnings from paying artists royalties for AI-generated art 144 upvotes, 118 comments
  26. Show HN: DenchClaw – Local CRM on Top of OpenClaw 126 upvotes, 102 comments
  27. Show HN: I Was Here – Draw on street view, others can find your drawings 39 upvotes, 27 comments
  28. DARPA’s new X-76 207 upvotes, 192 comments
  29. Yann LeCun's AI startup raises $1B in Europe's largest ever seed round 84 upvotes, 70 comments
  30. Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft 492 upvotes, 507 comments
